sections.heading: 1. Short Recap
sections.body: The New York Mets enter this game with a 3-3 record, having lost their last two games against the St. Louis Cardinals. The San Francisco Giants are 2-4, coming off a heavy loss to the San Diego Padres (1-7).

sections.heading: 2. Pitching Matchup
sections.body: David Peterson (Mets) has started the season strong with a 1-0 record and a 0.00 ERA over 5.1 innings. His stat line indicates a solid strikeout potential (12% K rate) despite a high WHIP of 1.50. In contrast, Robbie Ray (Giants) holds a 0-1 record and a 3.38 ERA, allowing a notable 1.69 HR/9 despite a decent WHIP of 0.94 and a higher strikeout rate (19%).

sections.heading: 3. Lineup Comparison
sections.body: The Mets' offense boasts a significantly higher wRC+ (94) compared to the Giants' 65, showing a stark disparity in offensive output. The Mets also demonstrate superior hard-hit (39.4% vs. 32.2%) and barrel rates (7.6% vs. 3.0%), while enjoying a better walk rate (11.9% vs. 7.1%) and lower strikeout rate (20.1% vs. 25.9%).

sections.heading: 4. Bullpen
sections.body: The Mets' bullpen has been strong this season with a 2.50 ERA and 1.18 WHIP. In comparison, the Giants' bullpen has a 3.74 ERA and a higher WHIP of 1.21, indicating that the Mets have an advantage in late-game situations.

sections.heading: 5. Key Edges
sections.body: The Mets hold a considerable advantage in various key metrics including wRC+ (94 vs. 65), hard-hit rate (39.4% vs. 32.2%), and walk rate (11.9% vs. 7.1%). Peterson’s control, coupled with the Giants’ struggles against left-handed pitchers (only 25.9% K rate), further favors the Mets.

sections.heading: 6. Today's Pick
sections.body: Considering the overall strength of the Mets' pitching and lineup, and the Giants' recent performance, today's pick is clearly in favor of the New York Mets.

writeup.pickSummary: Analyzing the metrics reveals that the Mets have distinct advantages in both pitching and hitting, favoring them strongly in today's matchup against the struggling Giants. With Peterson's dominant early performance and the overall offensive edge, betting on the Mets provides solid value.
writeup.officialPick: Today's Pick: New York Mets Moneyline

lastMeeting.summary: On 2026-03-31, the Mets lost to the St. Louis Cardinals 0-3 at Busch Stadium.
lastMeeting.recapHeadline: With Cards pitcher in a bind, Gold Glover's hose puts out the fire
recapNotes: For the second night in a row at Busch Stadium, a Cardinals starter pitched into the sixth inning but saw his outing screech to a halt before he could record an out in the frame.
Andre Pallante cruised through five innings before the heart of the Mets’ order started to get to him in the sixth.

source.label: Last meeting recap
source.headline: With Cards pitcher in a bind, Gold Glover's hose puts out the fire

sections.heading: 1. Short Recap
sections.body: Mets 3-2, St. Louis Cardinals 3-2, Busch Stadium. Last meeting: On 2026-03-31, the Mets lost to the St. Louis Cardinals 0-3 at Busch Stadium. Recap source: With Cards pitcher in a bind, Gold Glover's hose puts out the fire.

sections.heading: 2. Pitching Matchup
sections.body: Freddy Peralta (7.20 ERA, 1.20 WHIP, 2026 - 5.0 IP) vs Matthew Liberatore (1.80 ERA, 1.80 WHIP, 2026 - 5.0 IP).

sections.heading: 3. Lineup Comparison
sections.body: Lineups are confirmed. Team offense: NYM wRC+ 103, xwOBA 0.332, K% 21.4%. St. Louis Cardinals wRC+ 106, xwOBA 0.347, K% 19.5%.

sections.heading: 4. Bullpen
sections.body: Bullpen check: Mets ERA 2.87, xFIP 3.72, WHIP 1.26. St. Louis Cardinals ERA 5.09, xFIP 5.22, WHIP 1.65.

sections.heading: 5. Key Edges
sections.body: Main numbers: NYM wRC+ 103 vs 106, NYM xwOBA 0.332 vs 0.347, NYM bullpen rating 85 vs 69.

sections.heading: 6. Today's Pick
sections.body: Today's Pick: New York Mets Moneyline. Reason: better lineup quality and the stronger bullpen numbers.

writeup.pickSummary: Play the Mets moneyline. The case is simple: better team offense, cleaner bullpen profile, and a favorable recent series result.
